source_evidence: Section 4.1, Eq. (10): V_opt = [(n_f l_f)/sigma_T - (-n_f l_f)/sigma_C](2 pi r) = (sqrt(2)M/(4 pi r^2))(sqrt(2)H)[1/sigma_T + 1/sigma_C](2 pi r) = (MH/r)[1/sigma_T + 1/sigma_C]. 'A moment pair causes a pure-shear condition on the cylinder surface... principal stress lines oriented at +-pi/4.' Fiber force n_f = sqrt(2)M/(4 pi r^2), length l_f = sqrt(2)H.
purpose: Provides the analytical closed-form optimal (minimum) volume of a cylindrical truss transferring an end torsion moment, used to verify GRAND3 numerical results.
assumptions: Moment pair causes a pure-shear condition on the cylinder surface
Members follow the principal stress lines oriented at +-pi/4
Fiber tension force n_f = sqrt(2) M / (4 pi r^2), compression -n_f, fiber length l_f = sqrt(2) H
Domain fully supported on the bottom end cap; moment distributed over the outer ring of the upper end cap
formula_or_algorithm: V_opt = (M H / r) [1/sigma_T + 1/sigma_C]. Derived from V_opt = [(n_f l_f)/sigma_T - (-n_f l_f)/sigma_C](2 pi r) with n_f = sqrt(2)M/(4 pi r^2) and l_f = sqrt(2)H. For H=11, r=3, M=5: V_opt = 36.6667.
validation_opportunities: analytical solution (this closed form is the reference)
benchmark: GRAND3 converges to V_opt = 36.6667 (Table 2, Fig. 6)

reuse_risk.explanation: Simple explicit algebraic formula from a well-documented derivation; trivially reusable as a benchmark.
